properties.title: The AI Oracle
properties.description: 3D models of Oracle Bones from the British Library collections (early chat GPT ;) This wrapper html page was created with the help of Claude AI.

properties.title: Enlightening Wisdom
properties.description: One of the first Hindu texts translated into a european language. Discovered through the Qalamos portal a union catalogue of Asian and African script traditions in German memory institutions. Illustration features Nārada a sage-divinity famous in Hindu traditions as a travelling musician and storyteller who carries news and enlightening wisdom. The image quality device included in this image is a further translation from physical to digital.

properties.title: Gamle Bybro
properties.description: A poscard of the gamle bybro (old town bridge) in Trondheim

properties.title: Thor Bach - Working in the living room
properties.collection: NTNU (Norwegian University of Science and Technology)
properties.description: Two people in motion, working. Thor Bach (on the right) studied electrical engineering at NTNU, and was a resistance fighter during World War II.

properties.title: Kristiansten Fortress
properties.collection: NTNU (Norwegian University of Science and Technology)
properties.description: The picture shows buildings along the Nidelva, seen from Vollafallet. In the background Skrenten and the Winter Palace. Kristiansten Fortress is illuminated. Winter picture with snow.

properties.title: St Olaf at Sea
properties.description: The steam frigate "St. Olaf", photographed by Anders Beer Wilse in 1905.

properties.title: Trams and Palaces
properties.collection: NTNU (Norwegian University of Science and Technology)
properties.description: Stiftsgården, a royal palace, photographed by Anders Beer Wilse in 1902, is one of the largest wooden buildings in Northern Europe. The gardens behind it are now a public park.

properties.title: St Olaf statue
properties.description: The carved early 14th century sculpture of St Olaf, the patron saint of Norway. It originally stood in Bunge church on the Swedish island of Gotland.

properties.title: Harald Hardrada lands in York
properties.description: The landing of King Harald Hardrada near York, and the defeat of the Northumbrian army, depicted in the Life of St Edward the Confessor, c.1255.

properties.title: Harald Hardrada dies in battle
properties.description: The Battle of Stamford Bridge and the death of Harald Hardrada, depicted in the Life of St Edward the Confessor, c.1255.

properties.title: The world is a stage
properties.description: Explore and visualise the history of theatre through a curated collection of playbills and programmes.

properties.title: On the menu
properties.description: A dataset of 2,403 historical menu cards from the Berlin State Library, from about 1880 to 1913.

properties.title: On the menu
properties.description: Menu card from the steamship "Kaiser Wilhelm der Grosse"

properties.title: Troll Stone
properties.collection: NTNU (Norwegian University of Science and Technology)
properties.description: Meadow of wood anemone flowers by a stream in a spruce forest. "Troll-stone" in the foreground. A troll stone, in Norwegian folk lore, trolls turn to stone in sunlight.

properties.title: Railways & Rivers
properties.collection: NTNU (Norwegian University of Science and Technology)
properties.description: An image of the Flåmsbanen railway being built. It is wonderful how digitisation of images allow us to see past versions of places we currently visit.

properties.title: A Crowning for Trondheim Technical University
properties.collection: NTNU (Norwegian University of Science and Technology)
properties.description: A postcard of the 1906 coronation procession in Munkegata on the way to the Cathedral. People stand along the street in front of Trondheim Technical University, now the City Hall.

properties.title: High speed crown
properties.collection: NTNU (Norwegian University of Science and Technology)
properties.description: Picture of the coronation procession in 1906. People have gathered in the street. In the background you can see the shops at Olav Tryggvasons gate 39-41. "Berlitz School", and "Hartmann A/S Jernvareforretning". A horse-drawn carriage drives past at full speed, perhaps the royal couple were in it.

properties.title: Kristiansten Fortress
properties.description: Kristiansten Fortress is one of Trondheim’s main landmarks. The fort was built after the great city fire in 1681 and now stands guard over the city. The flag, which can be seen from most of the city, is raised when the fortress is open.

properties.title: Lykkens portal (Gate of Happiness)
properties.description: Gamle Bybro is also known as the ‘Portal of Happiness’ after the lyrics of the popular romantic waltz ‘Nidelven stille og vakker du er’, written by Oskar Hoddø.

properties.title: Nidaros Cathedral
properties.description: Nidaros Cathedral is the world’s northernmost Gothic cathedral and Norway’s national shrine. Built from the year 1070 at the tomb of St. Olav.

properties.title: Trafon (Old Electrical Exchange)
properties.description: Trafon in Trondheim, formerly the Vollan electrical distribution station, is an example of how important it is to build beautifully. Designed by architect Axel Guldahl senior, Trafon was built in 1910-1911 and was in operation until 1964. In 1993 it was taken into use by the student society, and is mainly used as offices during the UKA festival.

properties.title: Døvekirken (Deaf Church)
properties.description: The deaf church is the Norwegian Church’s nationwide offer for the deaf, hearing impaired and deaf-blind and sign language.

properties.title: NINA Huset
properties.description: NINA is an independent research foundation advancing knowledge on nature and the interplay between nature and society. The building was designed around the Norwegian landscape.

properties.title: Lerkendal station
properties.description: Lerkendal Station was opened on 1 December 1988 and is located in the immediate vicinity of the Gløshaugen campus. The station acts as the southern terminus of the Trøndelag Commuter Rail.

properties.title: NTNU Main Building
properties.description: The main building at the NTNU Gløshaugen campus is one of the first four buildings that was built for the Norwegian University of Technology (NTH). The building was inaugurated when the NTH opened in 1910.

properties.title: Site of Elgeseter Priory
properties.description: Traces of the Augustine monastery Elgeseter monastery have been identified in the area. The monastery is believed to have been founded in 1180 and was in use until it was destroyed in the second half of the 16th century. Are the remains of the Viking king Harald Hardrade under the pavement?

properties.title: Elgeseter bridge
properties.description: The concrete bridge that spans the Nidelva River, opened in 1951, has had a long history. The main entryway into Trondheim for hundreds of years has been at Elgeseter; the first bridge here is mentioned in 1178.

properties.title: Walk of Peace
properties.description: Walk of Peace is Trondheim’s “Walk of Fame” but with memorial stones to honour laureates of the Student Peace Prize instead of movie stars.

properties.title: Olaf Tryggvason statue sundial
properties.description: Keeping watch over the square is Trondheim’s founder the Viking King Olav Tryggvason. The statue was erected in 1921 and is made by the sculptor Wilhelm Rasmussen. The statue is actually a large sundial the column casting its shadow on a cobblestone clock face. The pedestal around the Olav statue is designed as a “water clock” that supplements the sundial.

properties.title: NR 25 (Phone Box Library)
properties.description: 100 telephone boxes remain in Norway, and some have been made into libraries or 'reading booths' - this is one of them! They were protected for their cultural importance in 2007. The red telephone booth has become a landmark and has its own national preservation plan. The booth is the result of an architectural competition announced by Oslo Telephone System in 1932, which was won by architect Georg Fredrik Fasting from Bergen.

properties.title: Trampe Bicycle Lift
properties.description: Opening in August 1993, this was the first and only bicycle lift in the world

properties.title: St. Olavs Gate Gråkallbanen
properties.description: The world’s northernmost tram line
